steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The first sentence states a geologic principle (intrusions are younger than the rocks they cut). The second sentence shows geochemists applying that principle to date a specific intrusion. The transition must signal that the second sentence follows logically from the first — a result or application of the stated rule.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Accordingly,: Means "therefore" or "in keeping with what was just said." It correctly signals that the geochemists' ability to date the intrusion follows directly from the stated principle. ✓
  • Choice B — That being said,: Signals a concession or contrast with the previous sentence. The second sentence does not contradict or qualify the principle — it applies it. ✗
  • Choice C — To this end,: Signals that the second sentence describes an action taken in order to achieve the goal mentioned in the first. The principle is not a goal to be achieved; it is a rule being applied. ✗
  • Choice D — Moreover,: Signals that the second sentence adds a new, separate point. The second sentence is not adding extra information but rather illustrating a consequence of the principle. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
